items.title: Police search abandoned mine shafts for missing woman Trisha Graf

children.content: Major Crime investigators have packed up after focusing on outback SA mine shafts in their search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf, who went missing last year.

articles.title: No evidence found on first day of new search for SA outback mother

children.content: SA Police say searches in the outback town of Andamooka on Wednesday failed to recover any evidence relating to the disappearance of missing local woman Trisha Graf.

image.altText: Two people sit in the entrance to a large hole with dirt around and a four-wheel drive

gallery.altText: A policeman has his back turned. The vast outback is in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A policeman has his back turned. The vast outback is in the background.

items.title: 'The premier can frack off': Government plans to remove fracking ban in SA's South East

children.content: The South Australian government is planning to lift a ban on fracking in the state's South East amid reports of increasing risks to gas supply in southern parts of the nation.

image.altText: Peter Malinauskas looks serious mid-speech

items.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform

children.content: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

image.altText: A man stands at a shop counter, guns are behind him

items.title: Fire at North Adelaide Golf Course suspicious, police say

children.content: Police say a fire destroyed a temporary structure often used at building sites and are calling for any witnesses who saw suspicious activity to come forward.

articles.title: Protesters demand 'stop the chop' at rally opposing North Adelaide golf project

children.content: More than 2,000 people gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

image.altText: Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.

gallery.altText: A temporary building at a building site gutted by fire

thumbnail.altText: A temporary building at a building site gutted by fire

items.title: Notorious paedophile's lawyer says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'

children.content: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

image.altText: Peter Liddy

items.title: Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' in budget as SA waits for freight bypass funding

children.content: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.

image.altText: A view from near the blast furnace of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA to prevent preterm birth

children.content: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

image.altText: A mother holds her baby in a lab

items.title: Woman stole parrot from pet shop by hiding it under top, police allege

children.content: South Australian police swoop on the alleged perpetrator of a parrot theft at a suburban Adelaide pet shop, but say the bird has not been found.

image.altText: A parrot police say was stolen.

items.title: Midnight Oil's Peter Garrett 'closes the circle' to chair Landcare

children.content: The former frontman for the Australian rockers was among those who helped lobby for the formation of Landcare Australia in 1989, so it seems fitting he should circle back to chair the organisation after a lifetime lobbying for the environment.

gallery.altText: man wearing hat stands beside tree

thumbnail.altText: man wearing hat stands beside tree

items.title: Duo swindled $6 million from five victims in investment scam, court hears

children.content: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.

image.altText: Bare branches covering a large beige building in the background

items.title: SA man forced to spend night in hospital ED due to lack of access cabs

children.content: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.

image.altText: A man sits in a mobility wheelchair.

items.title: Coles found to have misled shoppers in bombshell Federal Court case

children.content: Supermarket giant Coles broke consumer law by misleading shoppers on discount prices, a Federal Court judge has found.

articles.title: ASX falls, with Coles down -2.5 per cent on news it misled customers

children.content: The ASX 200 is down this afternoon, led by falls in IT and consumer staples. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in Deccember 2022.

gallery.altText: A close up of a red sign with Coles spelt out in white letters.
A red and white sign hangs over displays of fruit in a Coles supermarket and reads "great value, hands down"
ACCC’s legal counsel Garry Rich leaving court.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a red sign with Coles spelt out in white letters.
A red and white sign hangs over displays of fruit in a Coles supermarket and reads "great value, hands down"
ACCC’s legal counsel Garry Rich leaving court.

items.title: These charts show how Coles Down Down pricing patterns duped shoppers

children.content: The pricing patterns of these six products show how Coles was found to have misled consumers through its Down Down pricing patterns.

image.altText: A illustration mockup of a chart showing the Coles white label price vs the Down Down price.

items.title: Power to cut funding and stricter 'permanence' test under new NDIS

children.content: Legislation designed to save $35 billion in NDIS spending is introduced to parliament, revealing new powers for the minister to cut funding to classes of supports, automate some decisions and create requirements for prospective participants to exhaust all other treatment options before entering the NDIS.

image.altText: A close-up profile image of a man with short hair, wearing a suit. The background is dark.

items.title: Entire university sector accused of antisemitism failure

children.content: The special envoy on antisemitism has found not a single Australian university has met its requirements for adopting a definition of antisemitism.

image.altText: Great Court walkway at the University of Queensland.

items.title: Australian hantavirus ship passengers to depart for home today

children.content: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak are testing negative for the illness and are symptom-free, the federal health minister says, as they prepare to be repatriated to Australia.

image.altText: A plane on the tarmac at the airport surrounded by people in white body suits carrying white bags.

items.title: Antarctic expeditioner removed after weapon incident

children.content: A member of an Antarctic expedition who allegedly threatened colleagues with a bladed weapon has been removed from a Korean research station in an emergency evacuation.

image.altText: A screenshot of CCTV vision showing a man in a blue and black jacket wielding a knife, with his face blurred.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

image.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.

items.title: Arrested man a relative of Canberra stabbing victim, police say

children.content: Paramedics treated the woman for critical injuries at the scene but she was declared dead on her way to hospital.

image.altText: A residential street, police tape, and crime scene

items.title: Can you put sneakers in the washing machine? Here’s what the experts say

children.content: Experts reveal their tips on bringing your favourite sneakers back to life at home.

image.altText: A woman's hand holding sneakers near a washing machine

items.title: Blockbuster movies are connecting new audiences to classical music

children.content: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

gallery.altText: A man with bright blue eyes and dark curly hair reaching desperately for a gold ring.

thumbnail.altText: A man with bright blue eyes and dark curly hair reaching desperately for a gold ring.

items.title: Which country has won Eurovision the most? Take our More or Less quiz

children.content: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

image.altText: Kate Miller-Heidke is reaching towards the camera, suspended on a pole, with a microphone in opposite hand.

items.title: Taser-death inquest told officers had 'plenty of time' to deal with 95yo

children.content: A senior NSW Ambulance officer tells the Clare Nowland inquest that emergency services could have taken more time responding to the 95-year-old's dementia-related aggression.

image.altText: an elderly woman looking at the camera

items.title: Prime minister concedes aged care wait times 'not good enough'

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

image.altText: Albanese looking down in a suit while touching his ear.

items.title: Non-citizens to be banned from NDIS under Coalition policy

children.content: Non-citizens, including permanent residents, will be barred from accessing welfare under an updated migration crackdown promised by the Coalition.

image.altText: Profile of a man.

items.title: King opens UK parliament but doomed PM Starmer may not last the week

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister remains on life support amid mounting speculation he could face a leadership challenge as early as Thursday, local time.

A man on a throne wearing a crown holding a book and looking on
A man and a woman walking and smiling.

items.title: Alex Murdaugh murder convictions overturned

children.content: An infamous case will be retried after a US court ruled in favour of the South Carolina father.

image.altText: a man in orange jumpsuit and glasses clasps hands listening in court

items.title: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was

children.content: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

image.altText: Michael Voss with both fists raised in celebration

items.title: Oldest evidence of dentistry found in 59,000-year-old Neanderthal tooth

children.content: Archaeologists have found a hole in a 59,000-year-old tooth, which they say was drilled to treat a painful cavity. The find suggests Neanderthals could perform complex medical procedures.

image.altText: Museum diorama of Neanderthal family in a cave.

items.title: Investors, renters and home owners react to housing reforms

children.content: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.

image.altText: A woman sits with her legs crossed on a stone wall overlooking the ocean.

items.title: VAR drama, pitch invasion as Scottish league heads for final day shootout

children.content: Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho scores a controversial penalty with the last kick of the game to secure a 3-2 win at Motherwell, sending the Scottish Premiership title race to a final-day showdown against leaders Heart of Midlothian.

gallery.altText: Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho celebrates scoring a penalty, holding his jersey aloft, fans invade the pitch

thumbnail.altText: Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho celebrates scoring a penalty, holding his jersey aloft, fans invade the pitch
